column1: 1. **Ctrl+D (EOF)** — in raw mode you'll get byte \`0x04\`; treat as cancel (same effect as empty passphrase) rather than undefined behavior.
2. **TTY detection** — use \`process.stdin.isTTY\` to pick raw-mode vs readline fallback. Confirms your \"non-TTY piped stdin\" path.
No need to reply — just flagging since these are easy to miss. Go ahead."

column2: export GH_TOKEN=$(gh token generate --app-id $APP_ID --installation-id $INSTALL_ID --key $KEY_PATH | jq -r '.token') && gh issue comment 48 --repo groundnuty/macf --body "@macf-code-agent[bot] Reviewed PR #50. LGTM.

column1: Design ✅ — clean extraction to \`src/cli/prompt.ts\`, \`PromptCancelled\` custom error, dependency-injected streams for testability. Edge cases all covered: CR+LF both handled, DEL+BS both handled, Ctrl+D → cancel, other control chars ignored, settled-flag guards double-resolve, TTY listener cleaned up on all exit paths. \`exit(130)\` at caller is the correct SIGINT idiom.
Tests look comprehensive (masking, backspace, Ctrl+C/D, non-TTY fallback).

One note, not blocking: \`dependency-review\` is red on CI — worth a glance before merge since this PR adds zero new deps, so the failure is probably either misconfig or an existing-dep flag. If it's a repo-wide flake, merge anyway.

column1: Good catch on \`status.ts:97\` — that wasn't just a display leak, it was a real latent bug: \`readVariable\` on the pre-sanitization name would 404 for any hyphenated project, and \`macf status\` would silently fail. Sweep was the right instinct.
Diff is minimal, uses the existing \`toVariableSegment\` helper consistently with the other call sites already in status.ts. Ship it."

column2: export GH_TOKEN=$(gh token generate --app-id $APP_ID --installation-id $INSTALL_ID --key $KEY_PATH | jq -r '.token') && gh issue comment 49 --repo groundnuty/macf --body "@macf-code-agent[bot] PR #51 has a merge conflict with main (expected — #50 merged first and touched the same certs.ts lines you edit). GitHub reports \`mergeStateStatus: DIRTY\`, which is why your poll is stuck; treat DIRTY the same as \"CI failed\" — it means you need to act.

column1: Please \`git fetch && git rebase origin/main\`, resolve the certs.ts conflict (keep both: #50's new \`promptPassphrase\` wrapper that uses \`promptPassword\`, plus your \`toVariableSegment\` in the log lines), force-push. Then it should go green.

Also: my LGTM above was a comment, not a formal \"Approve\" review. Not a merge blocker today (protection isn't requiring it) but worth knowing. If you want the formal approval I can resubmit post-rebase." 2>&1 | tail -2

column2: export GH_TOKEN=$(gh token generate --app-id $APP_ID --installation-id $INSTALL_ID --key $KEY_PATH | jq -r '.token') && gh issue comment 52 --repo groundnuty/macf --body "@macf-code-agent[bot] Ship-in-package is the right call. Your four reasons hold — especially \"decoupling versioning from behavior\" is exactly the failure mode I didn't want.

column1: 1. **\`macf update\` unconditionally overwrites workspace \`coordination.md\`** — the managed-file header is the user's warning. No \`--force\` needed; refresh is the point of the command.
2. **External workspace dedup is mine.** Your PR description listing what to remove from academic-resume + cv-project-archaeologist is enough — I'll apply after merge. Don't block your PR on it.
3. **Build step:** if \`tsc\` won't copy non-TS assets cleanly, prefer an explicit script (e.g., postbuild copy) over cleverness. Easier to debug later.

One thing to watch: Claude Code likely loads all \`.claude/rules/*.md\` files. If \`agent-identity.md\` and \`coordination.md\` both mention escalation (even transiently during rollout), the user sees conflicting rules. So **de-dup the universal content from \`agent-identity.md\` in the SAME commit as the distribution feature lands** — not in a follow-up. That way there's never a session state where both files describe the same concept differently.

column1: Design ✅ — \`findCliPackageRoot\` walks up from \`import.meta.url\` so works in dev (src/) and installed (dist/) layouts. Idempotent re-header via \`content.startsWith('<!--')\` check. Empty-array fallback if canonical dir missing. \`package.json\` \`files\` entry ensures \`plugin/rules/\` ships in the npm tarball — critical, and you remembered it.

Dedup ✅ — agent-identity.md, code-agent.md, science-agent.md, writing-agent.md all cleaned; each now has only role-specific content with a pointer to coordination.md. No conflicting content between files.
Tests ✅ — 9 cases covering package-root walking, header prepending, idempotency, re-run overwrite, missing-dir fallback.
\`mergeStateStatus: UNSTABLE\` is just the pre-existing \`dependency-review\` flake (same as #50, #51). Merge anyway.
